name: RETROPERITONEAL FIBROSIS
definition: RPF = ORMOND DISEASE = CHRONIC PERIAORTITIS = PERIURETERITIS FIBROSA = GEROTA FASCIITIS SCLEROSING RETROPERITONEAL GRANULOMA uncommon collagen vascular disease of unknown cause characterized by aggressive confluent

demographics.Path: hard retroperitoneal plaque with ill-defined margins enveloping abdominal aorta, iliac

discriminating_features: tethering of aorta / IVC to vertebrae without anterior displacement

general: tethering of aorta / IVC to vertebrae without anterior displacement
usually mild uni- / bilateral pyelocaliectasis → hydronephrosis → renal failure (56-100%)
hypo- / isoechoic well-demarcated irregularly contoured homogeneous mass with smooth
varying degrees of uni- / bilateral hydronephrosis
CT: well-delimited irregular periaortic rindlike layer of soft tissue isoattenuating to muscle
contrast enhancement (during active inflammation of immature plaque)
MR: low to medium homogeneous signal intensity on T1WI
heterogeneous high SI on T2WI (DDx: malignancy)
early soft-tissue enhancement mirrors degree of inflammatory activity
little / NO enhancement during venous phase
NUC: little / NO gallium uptake in chronic fibrotic stage
PET: increased uptake of FDG paralleling degree of activity
may detect remote disease (multifocal fibrosclerosis, infectious / neoplastic disease, other
